name: Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop
name_original: Sendero de Sierra Morena por Real de la Jara — circular por el Castillo de las Torres y el Castillo Real de la Jara
description: Jog the Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop, an 8.5-mile route through dehesas and along the Cala river, featuring a mediev
region_intro: Jogging around El Real De La Jara offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Norte Natural Park, characterized by medium-mountain landscapes, extensive dehesas, and Mediterranean forests. The region features cork oaks, holm oaks, and gall oaks, providing varied and scenic backdrops for running. The Ribera del Cala river forms a natural boundary, with its banks offering routes through biodiverse areas. Hilly terrain with ascents and descents provides varied jogging routes, including panoramic views from points like Mirador Cerro del Ejido. Best jogging routes around El Real de la Jara The most popular jogging route is Via de la Plata — circular from Almadén de la Plata, an 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This historic path offers a long-distance route through diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.8 km) path. This route winds through the natural park, showcasing its characteristic dehesas and forests. Local runners also love the Castillo del Real de la Jara – Cala Riverside loop from El Real de la Jara, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Ribera del Cala riverbanks and past the medieval castle, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Jogging around El Real De La Jara is defined by dehesas, Mediterranean forests, and the Ribera del Cala river. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. Nearly 20 runners have used komoot to explore El Real de la Jara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around El Real de la Jara?
A: The running routes in El Real de la Jara, located within the Sierra Norte Natural Park, feature a diverse medium-mountain landscape. You'll encounter extensive dehesas (wooded pastures) and Mediterranean forests with cork oaks, holm oaks, and gall oaks. The area is naturally undulating with hills, providing varied routes with both ascents and descents, offering panoramic views from several points.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks or natural features?
A: Yes, many routes incorporate the region's rich history and natural beauty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Real de la Jara – Cala Riverside loop from El Real de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the medieval castle and along the Ribera del Cala riverbanks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013151112/via-de-la-plata-circular-from-almaden-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; also offers a long-distance run through diverse landscapes, following an ancient path.

Q: What are some of the natural attractions I might see while jogging in the area?
A: While running, you can discover several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Cala&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural boundary with high biodiversity. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy expansive vistas from viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/650365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/443512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Dehesa Valley&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013653705/sierra-morena-trail-gr-48-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through the natural park. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Real de la Jara – Cala Riverside loop from El Real de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in El Real de la Jara?
A: The Sierra Norte Natural Park, where El Real de la Jara is located, offers pleasant conditions for running during much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended if running during this season. Winter offers cooler, crisp air, perfect for invigorating runs.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while out on the trails?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its biodiversity, especially around the Ribera del Cala river. Keep an eye out for local wildlife such as otters, wildcats, wild boars, deer, and mouflons. The dehesas and Mediterranean forests provide habitats for a variety of species, making wildlife spotting a rewarding part of your run.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running routes in El Real de la Jara?
A: Yes, the hilly terrain of El Real de la Jara provides excellent opportunities for panoramic views. You can enjoy expansive vistas from points like the Mirador Cerro del Ejido, which offers views of the municipality and the distant Castillo de las Torres. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint loop from La Polana&lt;/a&gt; also provides stunning scenery.

Q: What kind of difficulty levels are available for running routes?
A: The routes around El Real de la Jara offer a mix of difficulty levels. While there are no easy routes listed in this guide, you'll find several moderate options, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013653705/sierra-morena-trail-gr-48-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking a greater challenge, there is also a difficult route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013151112/via-de-la-plata-circular-from-almaden-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via de la Plata — circular from Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near the running trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find several points of interest. The medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5587188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby, and you might spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5167019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Andalucía and Deer Statue&lt;/a&gt; in El Real de la Jara itself.

Q: How long are the typical running routes in this area?
A: The running routes vary in length to suit different preferences. You can find shorter loops, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31864036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Santa Olalla del Cala&lt;/a&gt; at approximately 5.5 km. Longer options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013653705/sierra-morena-trail-gr-48-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt; at about 13.8 km, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013151112/via-de-la-plata-circular-from-almaden-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via de la Plata — circular from Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 18 km long.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in El Real de la Jara?
A: Nearly 20 runners have used komoot to explore El Real de la Jara's varied terrain. They often praise the diverse landscapes, from the characteristic dehesas and Mediterranean forests to the scenic riverbanks of the Ribera del Cala. The mix of moderate and challenging routes, along with the historical and natural points of interest, makes for a highly enjoyable running experience.
tips: River that runs between the Sierra de Galaperosa and where it is right next to the road. Quiet, next to the water and ideal for running without any problems before arriving at the Ermita de Santa Eulàlia.
Although the ruins are located in the Badajoz municipality of Monesterio, the nearest town is El Real de la Jara, in the province of Seville. It stands on a rocky hillock that serves as its base.

The Real de la Jara Castle is located in the town of El Real de la Jara, in the province of Seville. This medieval castle occupies a strategic position atop a hill north of the town, making it an important defensive center during the Middle Ages.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in the village of El Real de la Jara. You can find parking within the village, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the loop.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through natural parks and agricultural areas (dehesas), it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and livestock, especially Iberian pigs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is during spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November). The weather is milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring rain, making some unpaved sections muddy.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need specific permits or pay entrance fees to jog this trail or to enter the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla. The trail is publicly accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Real de la Jara Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly unpaved, consisting of ancient paths through 'dehesas' and along the Rivera del Cala river. You can expect dirt paths, some sections of 'carril de arena' (sand track), and occasional stream crossings, especially after rain. About 90% of the route is natural surface.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience picturesque views of cork oaks and aromatic plants in the 'dehesa' ecosystem. Key highlights include the Cala Riverside, a Ford at Castillo de las Torres, and the historic Castillo del Real de la Jara.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered to have a low to moderate difficulty for hiking, and for jogging, it presents a moderate challenge due to its length and unpaved sections. Its gentle elevation profile makes it suitable for joggers with a reasonable level of fitness and active families looking for a longer outing.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the El Real de la Jara Loop?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this 13.75 km loop typically takes around 1 hour and 30 minutes to 2 hours, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the well-waymarked GR 48 trail. It also passes through sections of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, Mtb Vía de la Plata, and Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, some of which are part of the Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in El Real de la Jara, a village with Roman origins. You'll find cafes, pubs, and other facilities in the village before or after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the unpaved terrain and potential stream crossings, wear appropriate trail running shoes. Bring enough water, especially during warmer months, as there are no water sources directly on the trail. Sun protection and a basic first-aid kit are also recommended.
